mechanism_narrative: A high level of the federal funds rate increases the opportunity cost of equity investment and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity risk premium compression historically follows rate hikes
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level against 20-day forward returns remains above -0.02 or positive over any 2-year window, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, the labor market is considered fully tight, prompting anticipated monetary tightening and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical Fed tightening cycles often follow sub-4% unemployment
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4.0, or if the negative correlation dissipates over a rolling 3-year window (Rank IC > -0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive growth in retail sales and industrial production signals accelerating economic momentum that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Combined demand-supply expansion historically precedes short-term equity gains
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS growth * INDPRO growth) exhibits a 2-year rolling Rank IC below 0.03 or predicts wrong direction in over 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Within a high interest rate environment (federal funds rate above 5%), strong housing starts indicate resilient economic demand that can still support equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have performed as a leading indicator in high-rate periods
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS > 5%, HOUST's one-month change shows a negative Rank IC against 20-day returns over any 2-year window, or the positive signal rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of the Consumer Price Index erodes real purchasing power and forces consumer retrenchment, depressing corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation periods often correlate with lower real equity returns
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of CPIAUCSL level against 60-day forward returns is above 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period, or if the negative correlation fails to hold in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls signal accelerating job losses and a deteriorating economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised down.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change negative for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Two consecutive payroll declines historically precede equity downturns
failure_condition: If after two consecutive payroll declines the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combined effect of a low federal funds rate and high unemployment rate indicates accommodative monetary policy that directly supports risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Taylor-rule deviations have historically predicted equity returns
failure_condition: The interaction term (b1*FEDFUNDS + b2*UNRATE) shows a rolling 2-year Rank IC below 0.03 or a signal accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary regime defined by negative GDP growth, a recovery in retail sales signals an imminent economic trough and earnings rebound, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth negative for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales have been a reliable early-cycle indicator during past recessions
failure_condition: If during NBER-defined recessions with negative GDP growth, RSAFS month-over-month change has a Rank IC below 0.05 against 5-day returns, or if the positive prediction rate is below 55%.
